tipRules.tip: Yamamoto Zako is the go-to ChatterBait trailer
Yamamoto Hula Grub and Double Tail Grub are staples for football heads and as jig trailers
Howell (pro): Yamamoto/Daiwa Neko Straight 6.5-inch is excellent as drop-shot worm and for skipping under docks
Howell (pro): Daiwa/Yamamoto Neko Macho and 6.5-inch Neko Straight are made of same Senko material but unique designs give different action and fall rate
Howell (pro): 'Neko Macho is very versatile - fish it Texas-rigged on Daiichi Xpoint X-25 4/0 HD hook like a Senko and also wacky rig'
Hawk (pro): Yamamoto Stretch 40 grub works on Carolina rig, split shot, drop shot, weightless, and flipping

knowledge.insight: Hawk (pro): 'Stretch 40 is basically a 4-inch Senko with a small curly tail. The tail is small unlike most baits that size with large curl or ribbon tails - much more lively'

Card (pro): 'Single Tail Grub works all year but great during pre-spawn for smallmouth. Fish it like a small swimbait, reeling slow enough to just tick rocks. Also reel-reel-reel then let fall on tight line'
Card (pro): 'Reel slow enough that it just hits bottom every once in a while, barely ticking the rocks. Can also drag on bottom like a jig, or reel-reel-reel and let it fall on tight line'
Howell (pro): 'Daiwa/Yamamoto Neko worms are made of same material and formula as Senko but unique designs give different action and fall rate'
Card (pro): Yamamoto Single Tail Grub is also an excellent spinnerbait trailer
